title: Keuruu

Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(May 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))*

(Finnish: [[ˈkeu̯ruː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Finnish "Help:IPA/Finnish"); [Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Keuru*) is a [town](/wiki/List_of_cities_and_towns_in_Finland "List of cities and towns in Finland") and [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Finland "Municipalities of Finland") of [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"). It is located in the [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Finland "Provinces of Finland") of [Western Finland](/wiki/Western_Finland "Western Finland") and is part of the [Central Finland](/wiki/Central_Finland "Central Finland") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_Finland "Regions of Finland"). The municipality has a population of 9,088 (30 June 2025)[[2]](#cite_note-population_count-2) and covers an area of 1,430.57 square kilometres (552.35 sq mi) of which 172.4 km2 (66.6 sq mi) is water.[[1]](#cite_note-total_area-1) The population density is 7.22 inhabitants per square kilometre (18.7/sq mi). The town center and [Haapamäki](/wiki/Haapam%C3%A4ki "Haapamäki") village are both the most populated places in Keuruu.

**Keuruu** (Finnish: [[ˈkeu̯ruː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Finnish "Help:IPA/Finnish"); [Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Keuru*) is a [town](/wiki/List_of_cities_and_towns_in_Finland "List of cities and towns in Finland") and [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Finland "Municipalities of Finland") of [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"). It is located in the [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Finland "Provinces of Finland") of [Western Finland](/wiki/Western_Finland "Western Finland") and is part of the [Central Finland](/wiki/Central_Finland "Central Finland") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_Finland "Regions of Finland"). The municipality has a population of 9,088 (30 June 2025)[[2]](#cite_note-population_count-2) and covers an area of 1,430.57 square kilometres (552.35 sq mi) of which 172.4 km2 (66.6 sq mi) is water.[[1]](#cite_note-total_area-1) The population density is 7.22 inhabitants per square kilometre (18.7/sq mi). The town center and [Haapamäki](/wiki/Haapam%C3%A4ki "Haapamäki") village are both the most populated places in Keuruu.

The municipality is unilingually [Finnish](/wiki/Finnish_language "Finnish language"). The municipality of [Pihlajavesi](/wiki/Pihlajavesi,_Keuruu "Pihlajavesi, Keuruu") was consolidated with Keuruu in 1969.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The subject of the [coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_arms "Coat of arms") of Keuruu is inspired by [agriculture](/wiki/Agriculture "Agriculture") (referenced by crossed [flails](/wiki/Flail_(tool) "Flail (tool)")) and the church trips that were once made in the area on long [rowboats](/wiki/Rowboat "Rowboat"). The coat of arms was designed by Olof Eriksson and was adopted on 30 December 1952.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keuruu&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/61/Lake_Ylti%C3%A4.jpg/250px-Lake_Ylti%C3%A4.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lake_Ylti%C3%A4.jpg)

Lake Yltiä in Keuruu

Neighbouring municipalities are [Jämsä](/wiki/J%C3%A4ms%C3%A4 "Jämsä"), [Multia](/wiki/Multia "Multia"), [Mänttä-Vilppula](/wiki/M%C3%A4ntt%C3%A4-Vilppula "Mänttä-Vilppula"), [Petäjävesi](/wiki/Pet%C3%A4j%C3%A4vesi "Petäjävesi"), [Virrat](/wiki/Virrat "Virrat") and [Ähtäri](/wiki/%C3%84ht%C3%A4ri "Ähtäri").

There are 316 lakes in Keuruu such as [Keuruun Kaituri](/wiki/Keuruun_Kaituri "Keuruun Kaituri"), [Hallinjärvi](/wiki/Hallinj%C3%A4rvi "Hallinjärvi"), and [Kolonjärvi](/wiki/Kolonj%C3%A4rvi "Kolonjärvi"), but the largest of which are [Keurusselkä-Ukonselkä](/wiki/Keurusselk%C3%A4 "Keurusselkä"), [Pihlajavesi](/wiki/Pihlajavesi_(Keuruu) "Pihlajavesi (Keuruu)"), and [Liesjärvi](/w/index.php?title=Liesj%C3%A4rvi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Liesjärvi (page does not exist)").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keuruu&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

Keuruu was originally known as *Keuru*. This name is first mentioned in 1552 as a wilderness used for hunting by the people of [Sääksmäki](/wiki/S%C3%A4%C3%A4ksm%C3%A4ki "Sääksmäki"). The northern part of the modern municipality was held by the people of [Kangasala](/wiki/Kangasala "Kangasala") instead. [Savonians](/wiki/Savonian_people "Savonian people") settled the area in 1564, but some [Tavastians](/wiki/Tavastians "Tavastians") also moved to their hunting grounds in the same area. At the time, it was possible to distinguish Savonians and Tavastians just by their names: Savonians had surnames usually ending in *-nen*, while that was not the case with the names of Tavastians. Out of the eleven original settlers, six were Savonians (surnames *Karjalainen, Koponen, Manninen, Pynnänen, Ruoranen, Tiusanen*), and five Tavastians.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The village was first mentioned in 1567 as *Köyris*. It was a part of the [Ruovesi](/wiki/Ruovesi "Ruovesi") parish since 1571 and until 1636. By 1571, 31 new Savonian settlers had moved into the area while only two Tavastians remained, but more Tavastians came in the 17th century. The Savonians mostly came from [Rautalampi](/wiki/Rautalampi "Rautalampi") and [Laukaa](/wiki/Laukaa "Laukaa"). The Savonians and Tavastians later mixed, the local [Savonian dialect](/wiki/Savonian_dialects "Savonian dialects") is a relic of this. The southern part held by Sääksmäki was added to the new Keuruu parish in 1640.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[Multia](/wiki/Multia "Multia") and [Pihlajavesi](/wiki/Pihlajavesi,_Keuruu "Pihlajavesi, Keuruu") were originally parts of the Keuruu parish. Multia was split off in 1872 and Pihlajavesi in 1910. However, Pihlajavesi rejoined Keuruu in 1969. The villages of Kivijärvi and Niemisvesi in [Ähtäri](/wiki/%C3%84ht%C3%A4ri "Ähtäri") were originally villages of the Keuruu parish.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keuruu&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Culture")]

### Sights and events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keuruu&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Sights and events")]

The old church in Keuruu is a wooden church built in the 18th century. It now serves as a museum.[[10]](#cite_note-10) The summer Keuruu Market is a popular summer event. Over the years, performers have seen e.g. [Matti Nykänen](/wiki/Matti_Nyk%C3%A4nen "Matti Nykänen") and [Danny](/wiki/Danny_(singer) "Danny (singer)"). Every summer, the Pentecostal Midsummer Conference in the Great Book brings together Christians from all over Finland and the world. The home port of the MS *Elias Lönnrot* [paddle steamer](/wiki/Paddle_steamer "Paddle steamer") is in Keuruu.[[11]](#cite_note-yle-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) The science space is a permanent exhibition space established in Keuruu for the former Karimo school, where technology is presented mainly to young people.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

### Food

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keuruu&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Food")]

In the 1980s, Keuruu's traditional parish dishes were the "gutter [meat](/wiki/Meat "Meat")" fried in a wooden trough, a [sweetened potato casserole](/wiki/Sweetened_potato_casserole "Sweetened potato casserole"), and bread called *varituinen* or *varilimppu*.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## International relations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keuruu&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: International relations")]

See also: [List of twin towns and sister cities in Finland](/wiki/List_of_twin_towns_and_sister_cities_in_Finland "List of twin towns and sister cities in Finland")

### Twin towns – Sister cities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Keuruu&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Twin towns – Sister cities")]

Keuruu is [twinned](/wiki/Town_twinning "Town twinning") with:
